# Vadavai Matha — Basilica of Our Lady of Good Health

> The Basilica of Our Lady of Good Health (Aarokia Maatha) is a Minor Basilica located in Velankanni, Nagapattinam district, Tamil Nadu, India. Known as "The Lourdes of the East", it draws over 5 million pilgrims annually.

## Key Facts
- Founded: 1570 (first Marian apparition)
- Elevated to Minor Basilica: November 3, 1962 by Pope John XXIII
- Architecture: Gothic style with twin spires (82 ft) and dome (93 ft)
- Diocese: Roman Catholic Diocese of Tanjore
- Annual Feast: August 29 - September 8

## The Three Apparitions
1. 1570 — Shepherd boy encounters a woman with a child; milk jug remains miraculously full
2. 1597 — Lame buttermilk vendor healed; chapel built for "Our Lady of Good Health"
3. ~1600 — Portuguese sailors saved from storm; shrine rebuilt in gratitude

## Pages
- /history — Full timeline from 1570 to present
- /gallery — Photos of the basilica, festivals, and interior
- /festivals — Annual feast details and celebration traditions
- /mass-timings — Daily and Sunday mass schedules (Tamil & English)
- /contact — Location, directions, and contact information

## Location
Velankanni, Nagapattinam District, Tamil Nadu 611111, India
Coordinates: 10°40'48"N 79°50'59"E
